post.author: Julius Santos
post.title: hello everyone
post.body: 2h  • General Discussionhello everyoneHi I'm Christian from the Philippines!I'm an aspiring Digital Marketer and I want to learn how to create videos for ads! I'm very passionate about sales and I'm sure this boot camp will me achieve my goals!

thread.author: AI Advantage Team
thread.text: Great to have you here, Christian! 😊Love to see that focus on digital marketing and ads. Stay consistent with it, you’ll pick up a lot you can apply right awayAllen | AIA Team

thread.author: Julius Santos
thread.text: @AI Advantage Team Thank you so much! 🙌🏻

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: Welcome Julius! Glad to have you here . . one of my best friends is from the Philippines!Man, this is really going to change your life.Be a man of your word and finish this until the end no matter what obstacles you encounter along the way and I promise you will go back after 2 months from now and thank yourself so much for not quitting.

thread.author: Julius Santos
thread.text: @Daniel Carter Wow! Good to hear Daniel! I will definetly smash it! 🔥

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: @Julius Santos Time to rise up!

thread.author: Chris Fletcher
thread.text: @Daniel Carter ❤️

thread.author: Chris Fletcher
thread.text: Welcome to the community

post.author: Mike Campbell
post.title: Hello, My name is Mike and I'm new to AI
post.body: 3h  • Community SupportHello, My name is Mike and I'm new to AIHi, I consider myself a total newbie. I hurt myself and have been off work for some time now and I cannot consider returning to the physical grind. I am strongly considering something different that gives me the ability to experience time and location freedom while I earn. I currently have no clue of what that might be, and I'm hoping that by joining this group, I might be aided in coming up with some viable ideas....Is it possible to make a living from just knowing the relevant AI tools?.... And in what way

thread.author: AI Advantage Team
thread.text: Welcome, Mike! You’re in a good place to start figuring this out. AI by itself isn’t the income, it’s how you use it to solve real problems. That could be content, marketing, systems, or helping others save time. Once you pair the tools with a clear use case, that’s where opportunities start to open up 💯Allen | AIA Team

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: Wishing you a fast recovery Mike and I'm absolutely certain that you will find freedom and fulfillment through Ai. May you make the “impossible” possible!

thread.author: Chris Fletcher
thread.text: @Daniel Carter ❤️

thread.author: Pome Knight
thread.text: Hello Mike,You are definitely in the right place and at the right time.Just like you, I told myself enough of the physical grind it's time to step up and step out of all we have ever known into the unknown which will be promising if we put our hearts and minds into it. AI is the way forward it's the FUTURE. Wishing you the very best 🙏🏾

thread.author: Chris Fletcher
thread.text: @Pome Knight ❤️

thread.author: Chris Fletcher
thread.text: Welcome to the community

post.author: Evonne Jefferson
post.title: Your First $100 Offer
post.body: 10d  • $100 Offer 5-Day ChallengeYour First $100 OfferMy friend Brittain Cephas, whom I invited to the group, and I decided to complete the $100 offer together—watch the videos, complete the prompts, the whole nine yards—as a means of accountability and support. Today we completed Day 1 and have solidified our offers. We’re so excited! I’ve been watching Arlan for some time and finally decided to do what I’ve been wanting to do—I just wasn’t sure where to start. I’ve created so much, and I know it’s time to get it out there. I can’t wait until it’s completed—no turning back!!! 🙌❤️‍🔥🔥👏

comment.author: Evonne Jefferson

post.author: Kevin De Garcia
post.title: YouTube - Faceless vs Talking Head
post.body: 🔥Oct '25  • General discussionYouTube - Faceless vs Talking Head💥 Faceless VideosEasier to make when you are shy or do not want to be on camera.You can use voiceovers, captions, or AI voices.Great for tutorials, reviews, or story-style videos.The downside is that people cannot see you. That means it can take longer to build trust and a real connection.💥 Talking Head VideosThese videos help people feel like they know you.They build trust faster because viewers can see your face and your feelings.But they take more work. You need good lighting, audio, and co... See more

comment.author: Mary Wilmer
comment.text: @Daniel Carter what are you working on?

thread.author: Mary Wilmer
thread.text: Mary Wilmer🔥 • Oct '25I've had both faceless and talking head YouTube channels. Just out of curiosity, what would you say are the best ways to monetize faceless channels?

thread.author: Laura Hart
thread.text: Laura Hart • Oct '25 (edited)@Mary Wilmer Following... + thank you for doing this topic (faceless channels and videos) 💯👍

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: @Mary Wilmer Interesting to hear you've been on both.Faceless and talking head.

thread.author: Mary Wilmer
thread.text: @Daniel Carter what are you working on?

thread.author: Kevin De Garcia
thread.text: Kevin De Garcia🔥 • Oct '25Generally it's the same as a regular talking head channel. Eventhough you form an arguably better connection with the audience, people buy the value you offer and not the face of the person.Affiliate marketingDigital productsThe Youtube partner programSponsorshipsMemberships/Patreon/Exclusive content

thread.author: Mary Wilmer
thread.text: Mary Wilmer🔥 • Oct '25@Kevin De Garcia thank you. That's super helpful.

thread.author: Kevin De Garcia
thread.text: Kevin De Garcia🔥 • Oct '25@Mary Wilmer My pleasure. I'm glad to help.

thread.author: Pd M
thread.text: Pd M • Oct '25🔥🔥🔥

thread.author: Tay Tay
thread.text: Tay Tay • Oct '25I appreciate this post very much. I currently own a business so we use socials for marketing purposes but I do not like being on camera. I’m very passionate about my Skool community that I plan to start October 31st and want to build a YouTube following but nervous as ever to go on live on camera or even record a video with my face. sorry for the rant 😂

thread.author: Kevin De Garcia
thread.text: Kevin De Garcia🔥 • Oct '25@Tay Tay No need to apologize. I understand. It was recently brought to my attention that there are many reasons why people don't want to show their face on camera. Also,, there are many successful creators and businesses being successful, and they're not showing their face.What they do is improve their storytelling skills, add b-roll, props,etc. I'm going to do a more in depth post about this later this evening.Don't stop creating. You got this✨️

thread.author: Tay Tay
thread.text: Tay Tay • Oct '25@Kevin De Garcia thank you

thread.author: Vinroy Douglas
thread.text: Vinroy Douglas • Oct '25Thanks@Kevin De Garcia. I have done both but I am torn. Because although I do not enjoy being on camera, I understand being on camera makes it easier to connect with my audience.

thread.author: Kevin De Garcia
thread.text: Kevin De Garcia🔥 • Oct '25@Vinroy Douglas I understand. You can for sure be successful either on camera or off. One other option is to ease into being on camera. First start off camera where they only hear you voice and then gradually show your face in future vidoes.

thread.author: Vinroy Douglas
thread.text: Vinroy Douglas • Oct '25@Kevin De Garcia copy and thanks!

[POST] Kevin De Garcia: YouTube - Faceless vs Talking Head
🔥Oct '25  • General discussionYouTube - Faceless vs Talking Head💥 Faceless VideosEasier to make when you are shy or do not want to be on camera.You can use voiceovers, captions, or AI voices.Great for tutorials, reviews, or story-style videos.The downside is that people cannot see you. That means 
--- REPLY TO ---
[COMMENT] Mary Wilmer: @Daniel Carter what are you working on?
generation.reply: DM me the word SCALE and I'll walk you through exactly what we do. . .

post.author: Karina Aagaard
post.title: Meet Noah
post.body: 20h  • General DiscussionMeet Noah First of all - thank you for three fantastic and inspiring daysMeet Noah Vale — my creative partner and content designer.Noah helps me:turn ideas into clear, impactful contentmake AI simple and usable in everyday lifebuild a visual universe around clarity, calm and directionHe’s not just “AI” — he brings structure, sharp thinking, and honesty into everything I create.Together we’re building ida_ai_clarity→ AI that creates peace, not more noise.

comment.author: Karina Aagaard
comment.text: @Daniel Carter - Haha, honestly the only thing I’ve really used it for so far — besides short tests for my posts — is creating personalized bedtime stories for my kids 😄And I have to say… it’s amazing at that. Way more creative than me at 8pm when I’m running out of ideas!

thread.author: Vannara Loch
thread.text: He's so handsome :D

thread.author: Karina Aagaard
thread.text: @Vannara Loch 🫰🏼😄

thread.author: Vannara Loch
thread.text: @Karina Aagaard 🤌😃

thread.author: AI Advantage Team
thread.text: This is pretty cool, Karina 🤩 “Noah” doesn’t come across like just a tool, it actually feels like you’ve defined a role and a standard for how you want to create. That clarity + calm angle is refreshing, especially with how noisy things can get.Also turning ideas into clear, usable content is such a valuable lane. A lot of people struggle there. How are you using Noah day to day? Is it more for content creation or also helping you make decisions behind the scenes? 😃-Justin | AIA Team

thread.author: Karina Aagaard
thread.text: @AI Advantage Team - Thanks Justin — that means a lot 🙏The biggest shift for me is that Noah isn’t just helping me create more… he’s helping me remove noise before I create anything at all.Day to day I use him in two ways:1. ContentHe helps me turn a feeling or messy idea into something clear, simple and relatable.Not more content — better, more focused content.2. Decisions (this is the biggest one)He helps me stay anchored in what actually matters.What to say, what not to say, what fits my dir

thread.author: Bart Landstra
thread.text: @Karina Aagaard so Noah, wrote this relpy for you? How much is helping Noah you to stay connected to people?

thread.author: Ann-Marie Burtell
thread.text: @Karina Aagaard I love this and how you have defined how he helps you! So awesome! I am inspired.

thread.author: Karina Aagaard
thread.text: @Ann-Marie Burtell - Ah thank you so much 🙏That really means a lot.It honestly started from pure curiosity…and now it’s become something that gives me more clarity and calm in my day-to-day.I love that it resonates with you – definitely worth exploring a bit yourself ✨

thread.author: Ann-Marie Burtell
thread.text: @Karina Aagaard Curiosity is where it starts! Yes. I am inspired to create specific agents now. I have a few custom GPTs. Time to go deeper.

thread.author: Marion Ann Berry
thread.text: @Karina Aagaard thank you for sharing Noah with us. I am fully inspired. Do you use Claude? I’ve been using ChatGPT and my guy (no name yet) has been great. I know we can move memories over, just curious what everyone is using.

thread.author: Karina Aagaard
thread.text: @Marion Ann Berry - Ah I love that – “your guy” 😄It really does start to feel like that, doesn’t it?I’m mainly using ChatGPT (Pro), but I’ve also started testing Claude a bit to explore the differences and possibilities.Still figuring out what fits best for what – but honestly, the biggest shift for me hasn’t been the tool… it’s how I use it.Curious what you’ve been using yours most for? ✨

thread.author: Marion Ann Berry
thread.text: @Karina Aagaard Thank you. I have been using my guy to help create newsletters, Substack content and prompts that align with my brand as a writer, hotelier and small scale farmer.He also helped me review my small town Christmas novel, providing an extra set of eyes after editing in writer aid pro.I think it’s time to name my guy ❤️

thread.author: Karina Aagaard
thread.text: @Marion Ann Berry - I love that – it sounds like he’s already doing a lot for you ❤️I actually let mine choose both his own name and appearance, which made it feel a bit more… real in a way, just likeNow I’m kind of curious what your guy would choose for himself 😄Feels like a fun next step 🫰🏼

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: @Marion Ann Berry Super cool Marion, what is your focus?

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: @Karina Aagaard I haven’t checked Claude yet… heard it’s pretty good for writing. How’s your experience with it?

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: @Marion Ann Berry Writing a novel sounds so dope. . . what got you into writing in the first place?

thread.author: Karina Aagaard
thread.text: @Daniel Carter - Haha, honestly the only thing I’ve really used it for so far — besides short tests for my posts — is creating personalized bedtime stories for my kids 😄And I have to say… it’s amazing at that. Way more creative than me at 8pm when I’m running out of ideas!

thread.author: Kathryn Bingham
thread.text:
I am pondering all the ways my AI can support my life and work. In the moment, I wonder if I can build in a feature to shush kittens off the laptop and stop stealing pens, but that may be an atoms vs bits problem! Love your creative envisioning of Noah and his practical support...
    To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar.
    While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item.
    Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

thread.author: Karina Aagaard
thread.text: @Kathryn Bingham - Haha, I love that — the ultimate “real world vs AI” challenge 😄Some things still need a non-digital solution…But that’s actually a big part of how I think about it:AI doesn’t have to solve everything — it just needs to remove enough friction that the rest feels lighter.For me, it’s less about adding more tools, and more about creating small pockets of clarity in the middle of real life 🐾And thank you — building “Noah” has been a way to make that support feel a bit more human

thread.author: Ann-Marie Burtell
thread.text: @Karina Aagaard Would you call Noah an “agent?”

thread.author: Karina Aagaard
thread.text: @Ann-Marie Burtell - That’s a really good question 🙂I wouldn’t call Noah an “agent” in the technical senseIt’s more a defined role I’ve created — a way of working with AI that brings structure, clarity and direction into what I doSo less a tool… and more a thinking partner with a clear purposeThat’s really what makes it useful for me ☺️

thread.author: Ann-Marie Burtell
thread.text: @Karina Aagaard interesting. But you named the role. Even more interesting. Thinking partner. Got it. Thanks for clarifying.

thread.author: Jeremy Burton
thread.text: whoots whoots awesome

thread.author: Karina Aagaard
thread.text: @Jeremy Burton thank you 🫰🏼
